jobs.title: Postdoctoraal onderzoeker Postdoctoral Researcher Phytoplankton Metacommunity dynamics The University of Groningen invites applications for a Postdoctoral Researcher position within the UTOPIA project (Understanding Trait composition Of coastal meta-communities: Processes and Interactions Across organism groups). UTOPIA is funded by an open competition NWO Weave grant and is a collaboration between the Universities of Oldenburg and Groningen. Metacommunity ecology has been a major developmental stage in our understanding of how spatial dynamics and local species interactions shape community assembly, temporal and spatial biodiversity change and emergent functional properties at local and regional scales. At its base, metacommunity frameworks comprise three major aspects: the density-independent response of organisms to abiotic environmental gradients, the density-dependent species interactions in local patches (competition, trophic interactions, mutualism), and the dispersal between patches. The first captures the ecological niche, the second the constraints of local community ecology, and the last the passive and active movement of individuals in space. Combining these multiple trait dimensions with the comprising approach of metacommunity theory should fulfil “a mechanistic Utopia” as it allows us to fully explain the spatial and temporal occurrence of species. Reality, however, is that we can only explain a fraction of species composition by environmental and spatial variables. Utopia addresses this problem by a combination of model development, data analysis and experimentation that allows us to address the influence of Effective Assembly Rates (EAR) on meta-community composition along spatial and temporal gradients. We chose the Wadden Sea UNESCO World Heritage Site as our main region of interest as it combines a wealth of information on temporal and spatial community composition with a meta-community structure influenced by the chain of barrier islands from Western Netherlands to Denmark, by tidal basins and the major incoming rivers Ems, Weser, and Elbe. Due to its high protection status, it is also exposed to a range of natural dynamic processes that are rare to find in central European land- or seascapes. The postdoctoral researcher will join the Eriksson Lab Group and play a central scientific role in the UTOPIA project, with a focus on analysis of field-data from the Wadden Sea in a metacommunity dynamics perspective, and mesocosm based experimentation using phytoplankton communities. They will also play a central collaborative role in the research group, with the opportunity to be engaged in all other research lines. jobs.title: Promovendus PhD position Co-Designing Circular Economy Vocational Training in the RECEBA Project Join a groundbreaking EU Project at the intersection of Circularity, Education, and Industry Innovation! The RECEBA (Regional Circular Economy Business Acceleration) project is a flagship EU-funded initiative aimed at transforming Vocational Education and Training (VET) to support the transition to a circular economy. As part of this project, the University of Groningen/Campus Fryslân is leading the Work Package ‘Co-Designing & Delivering Circular Economy VET Programmes’. In collaboration with NHL Stenden University of Applied Sciences, which participates in the recruitment and supervision of the PhD project, we are seeking a motivated PhD candidate to contribute to this interdisciplinary, impact-driven research. This PhD position offers a unique opportunity to: Engage in applied research with real-world impact on sustainability, education, and regional economic development. Collaborate with a diverse, international consortium of academics, industry leaders, and policymakers. Design and research innovative pedagogical models, with Challenge-Based Learning at the core, to bridge the gap between theory and practice in circular economy education. Enhance your knowledge on theory and practice of circular economy. Publish in high-impact journals and present findings at international conferences. If you are passionate about sustainability, challenge-based learning, educational innovation, and systemic change, this PhD project will allow you to shape the future of circular economy skills for SMEs development while advancing your academic career. jobs.title: Promovendus PhD Docentprofessionalisering inzet AR/VR in het STEM-onderwijs Augmented reality (AR) en virtual reality (VR) zijn leertechnologieën die enorme potentie hebben om leeruitkomsten, attitudes en carrière-aspiraties van leerlingen voor Science, Technology, Engineering en Mathematics (STEM) te verbeteren. Vooral wanneer deze technologieën gebruikt worden om innovatief en effectief STEM-onderwijs te realiseren. Maar dat vraagt wel wat van de leerkracht, want deze moet doelgerichte lessen ontwerpen waarin AR/VR en STEM-didactieken elkaar betekenisvol versterken. In het project Adding a Layer to Learning hebben we samen met leraren en experts een docentprofessionalisering ontwikkeld, zodat leraren op doelgerichte en geïntegreerde wijze een AR/VR-laag kunnen toevoegen aan hun STEM-onderwijs. We onderzoeken de effecten van deze professionalisering op de vaardigheden van leraren, maar ook op de leeruitkomsten, attitude en carrière-aspiraties van leerlingen. jobs.title: Postdoctoraal onderzoeker Postdoctoral researcher in Speech Technology for Low Saxon varieties Within the Center for Groningen Language and Culture (CGTC) we are looking for a postdoctoral researcher (0.8 FTE, 25 months) on speech technology for Low Saxon varieties. The goal of this postdoctoral position is to investigate how low-resource speech technology can be developed and improved, specifically focusing on the various Dutch Low Saxon varieties. Consequently, this position will entail doing high-quality research into how low-resource varieties can benefit optimally from the most recent language and speech technology advancements and/or developing new approaches for this purpose. The position also entails helping (to supervise) PhD students with the development and improvement of automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems or text-to-speech (TTS) systems, as well as making these available for public use. The implemented technology will be made publicly available and may also be used in the MOI Grunnen | Drenthe project (www.wijzijnmoi.nl). In this project, learning material and an integrative approach is developed for teaching primary school children the regional language. Additional tasks for the successful applicant may involve helping to collect acoustic or textual data for these varieties (e.g., using custom online interfaces, or using our state-of-the-art mobile laboratory SPRAAKLAB), and attempting to acquire external research funding benefiting both the project and personal development. You will also collaborate with several PhDs and a postdoc working specifically on the MOI Grunnen | Drenthe project. You will become a member of both the GroNLP group and Speech Lab Groningen and as such, willingness to help with public engagement activities is also desirable. Deze vacature is alleen beschikbaar in het Engels Faculteit der Letteren 0.8 fte 2 augustus 2026 €4.241 - €4.728 >

jobs.title: Promovendus PhD position Multi-scale modelling of precipitate strengthening For the National Growth Fund (NGF) project “Groeien met Groen Staal” (GGS), a PhD position for the period of 4 years is available in the context of modelling green steel mechanics from the atomic scale. In this project, the University of Groningen will investigate micro-scale and atomic-scale processes of dislocation-precipitate interactions for multi-component alloy systems that are relevant for the new green steels compositions, where precipitates can occur due to impurities and tramp elements. The goal of the project is to devise a multi-scale framework where atomic-scale mechanisms of precipitate shearing/looping are incorporated into micro-scale framework of dislocation-precipitate interactions. This model will enable to predict precipitate strengthening as a function of the green steel composition. The successful candidate will be supervised by prof. dr. Francesco Maresca, chair of the Mechanics of Materials unit of the Engineering and Technology institute Groningen (ENTEG).
